name: Aroon(25) Oscillator>0 TQQQ
description: Holds leveraged Nasdaq when QQQ's Aroon oscillator (25-period AroonUp minus AroonDown) is positive, indicating that the most recent 25-day high is more recent than the most recent 25-day low. Exits to T-bills when the oscillator turns negative.
overfit_notes: Single Aroon(25) oscillator at zero-cross — textbook period and canonical threshold.

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open (only on regime change)

name: Aroon(25)>0 + ATR<2.5% TQQQ
description: Combines the Aroon(25) oscillator with a daily volatility gate based on ATR(14)/price. Holds leveraged Nasdaq only when Aroon is positive (uptrend) and current volatility is below 2.5%, sidestepping choppy high-volatility regimes.
overfit_notes: Aroon(25) + ATR(14)/price < 0.025 — two combined indicators; the 2.5% volatility threshold is slightly tuned but plausible.

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open (only on regime change)

name: CMO(20)>0 TQQQ
description: Uses a 20-period Chande Momentum Oscillator on QQQ to distinguish net-bullish from net-bearish regimes. CMO measures the ratio of upward to total price movement over the lookback window; a positive reading means up-days dominated and the algo holds leveraged Nasdaq.
overfit_notes: Single CMO(20) at zero-cross — standard 20-day lookback, canonical threshold.

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: CMO(20) > 0 (more up-day movement than down-day): 100% TQQQ

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open (only on regime change)

name: ROC(20)>0 Monthly TQQQ
description: Uses the 20-day Rate of Change on QQQ as a simple trend gate. If QQQ is higher than it was 20 trading days (≈1 month) ago, the regime is bullish and the algo holds TQQQ; otherwise it holds T-bills.
overfit_notes: Single ROC(20) at zero-cross — 20-day lookback maps to a standard monthly momentum window.

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open (only on regime change)
